title: Public MDX Developer Documentation Tasks
principles: Public docs are task-first and developer-facing; architecture docs remain the durable internal boundary source.
MDX examples must use current public SDK/API names and canonical schema-owned values.
Generated API reference should come from OpenAPI or package-owned schema metadata where possible.
Contribution docs must route external PR authors to the owning package, required source evidence and verification gates.
Docs-only slices require explicit no-changeset rationale; package-facing README/export/runtime changes require a Changeset.
Every docs-writing task must require implementers to read and thoroughly follow the documentation standards suite, including the relevant template file and user journey file.

globalVerification.commitPolicy: Commit each coherent docs slice after parent review and passing verification. Do not batch unrelated docs app, SDK docs and contribution docs changes into one oversized commit.
requiredBeforeFinalPR: docs app build/typecheck once apps/docs exists
local link audit for public docs pages
SDK/API code example smoke tests or explicit tracked follow-up for examples that cannot yet run

Documentation standards review covering reader journey, page template, voice, Australian spelling, sentence-case headings, no marketing language, current SDK/API names and canonical schema/type references
bun run changeset or explicit no-changeset rationale
evidenceRequired: Current and final docs request/build call graph
List of generated vs hand-written reference pages
Changeset path and release-train impact, or no-changeset rationale

Documentation standards review covering reader journey, page template, voice, Australian spelling, sentence-case headings, no marketing language, current SDK/API names and canonical schema/type references

tasks.title: Choose MDX docs app structure and navigation contract
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list.

Before editing:

- Read `docs/standards/documentation-style.md` and thoroughly follow it.
- Read `docs/standards/documentation-writing.md` for voice, tone, wording and editing rules.
- Read `docs/standards/documentation-templates.md`, then read the specific template file under `docs/standards/documentation-templates/` for every page type you write or review.
- Read `docs/standards/documentation-review.md` and use its audit checklists before handoff.
- Read `docs/standards/documentation-architecture.md` for docs ownership, MDX structure and source-of-truth rules.
- Read `docs/standards/documentation-user-journeys.md`, then read the specific journey file under `docs/standards/documentation-user-journeys/` for every target reader you write for.
- Identify the primary reader, primary need, page type, template, canonical source of truth, required examples, required diagrams and validation before writing content.
- Read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in `docs/architecture/*`.
- Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch.
- Review the spec's call-graph diagrams before editing, and update/report them if implementation discovers a different final graph.

Implementation rules:

- MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: `Data`, `Schema`, `Array`, `Chunk`, `HashSet`, `HashMap`, `Match`, `Context`, `Layer`, `Config`, `Service`, `Record`, `Result`, `Exit`, `Bun`, `Platform`, `Command` and `ManagedRuntime`.
- Do not fall back to plain TypeScript control flow, nullable values, mutable records, mutable arrays, `switch`, `Object.values`, `Object.entries`, ad hoc `_tag` objects, unsafe casts, stringly branching, custom env parsing or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect/schema/platform primitive fits.
- Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package.
- Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists.
- Keep one-off Effect error handling and transformations inline at the callsite. Do not extract tiny mapper/wrapper helpers for single-use `Effect.mapError`, `Effect.catchTag`, `Effect.catchAll` or `Effect.catchAllDefect`.
- Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe API/SDK exports instead of importing server-only internals.
- Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with `bun run changeset`, using a user-facing changelog summary. If the task is not package-facing, state that explicitly in the handoff.

Verification and handoff:

- Run this task's mandatory verification gates, including `bun run verification` unless the task explicitly documents a narrower gate.
- Report the Changeset path and release-train impact, or report why no Changeset was required.
- Report changed files, verification commands, outcomes and residual risks.
- Report whether the final implementation still matches the spec's call graph.
- Do not start or delegate another task. The parent agent must review, audit, verify and explicitly accept this task before the next task begins.

Task-specific scope: choose and document the MDX docs content location and navigation contract for Start, SDK, API, Guides, Concepts, Contributing and Reference. If scaffolding `apps/docs`, add the smallest framework/package setup needed for MDX build/typecheck. If not scaffolding yet, add the content tree and navigation config contract with explicit follow-up.
outputs: apps/docs/** or chosen MDX content root
docs/product-specs/public-mdx-developer-docs.md if implementation discovers a different final docs graph
README or docs app package README
mandatoryVerification: rg audit proving the navigation includes Start, SDK, API, Guides, Concepts, Contributing and Reference
bun run changeset or explicit no-changeset rationale
completionCriteria: The MDX content root and navigation ownership are clear.
The docs request/build call graph is documented.
Public docs remain generic and avoid private downstream product references.
Parent agent accepted the docs framework/content-root decision before content pages are bulk-authored.

Parent agent reviewed the docs against the documentation standards suite, including reader journey, template, voice, Australian spelling, sentence-case headings, no marketing language, current SDK/API names and canonical schema/type references.

Task-specific scope: write the public MDX Start and SDK pages: overview, quickstart, install, first calculation, SDK-vs-API decision page, plain SDK, safe SDK, Effect SDK, schemas and type safety. Use current public names and canonical branded values. Add runnable or typechecked example snippets where feasible.
outputs: SDK example smoke/typecheck files if needed
mandatoryVerification: docs app typecheck/build if apps/docs exists
SDK example smoke/typecheck command or explicit follow-up
rg audit proving SDK docs use WhatTax.calculate, WhatTax.safe.calculate, calculateRunRequest, calculateReportRequest, calculateReport and CalculatorRun* names
bun run changeset or explicit no-changeset rationale
completionCriteria: A developer can run a first SDK calculation from the quickstart.
Plain SDK is the beginner path and Effect SDK is documented as advanced/in-process transport usage.
Type safety docs explain canonical facts, branded values and runtime schema decoding.
Examples avoid stale names such as calculateRequest and createEffectClient.

Parent agent reviewed the docs against the documentation standards suite, including reader journey, template, voice, Australian spelling, sentence-case headings, no marketing language, current SDK/API names and canonical schema/type references.

Task-specific scope: write API overview/errors/OpenAPI routing pages, developer guides and concept pages. API docs must explain HTTP as a thin transport over the SDK/calculators boundary and should use generated OpenAPI reference where available instead of hand-written endpoint mirrors.
outputs: OpenAPI/reference integration or explicit generated-reference follow-up
mandatoryVerification: docs app typecheck/build if apps/docs exists
OpenAPI generation/reference smoke check or explicit follow-up
rg audit proving API docs include the HTTP calculate over SDK call graph
bun run changeset or explicit no-changeset rationale
completionCriteria: API docs explain when to use HTTP instead of the SDK.
Guides include prerequisites, complete examples, expected output and error handling.
Concept pages explain calculators, facts, rules, branded values, reports and diagnostics without becoming internal manuals.
Endpoint reference has a generated-source plan or implementation.

Parent agent reviewed the docs against the documentation standards suite, including reader journey, template, voice, Australian spelling, sentence-case headings, no marketing language, current SDK/API names and canonical schema/type references.

outputs: Examples MDX pages or example files
active exec plan validation evidence when implementation begins
mandatoryVerification: docs app typecheck/build if apps/docs exists
SDK/API example smoke tests or explicit accepted exclusions
OpenAPI/reference generation smoke check or explicit accepted exclusion
rg audit proving no private downstream product names in public docs
bun run changeset or explicit no-changeset rationale
completionCriteria: Reference pages exist for SDK, schemas, errors, API, changelog/versioning and release notes.
Examples cover Node/server, browser HTTP, Effect and error handling paths.
Final docs navigation matches the spec or the spec is updated with the accepted final structure.
Final validation evidence is recorded and public docs contain no private downstream product details.

Parent agent reviewed the docs against the documentation standards suite, including reader journey, template, voice, Australian spelling, sentence-case headings, no marketing language, current SDK/API names and canonical schema/type references.
